overrides.override_summary: Maximum allowable interest rate capped at 10% per annum
overrides.why_it_matters: Reduces late payment interest from 18% (1.5%/mo) to 10%

overrides.override_summary: NC choice of law and venue may not apply under CA law
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisee may be able to litigate under CA law and in CA courts

overrides.override_summary: Release excludes CA Franchise Investment Law (Corp Code 31000-31516) and Franchise Relations Act (B&P Code 20000-20043)
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisee retains right to bring claims under CA franchise statutes even when signing general release

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages clauses may be unenforceable under Civil Code Section 1671
overrides.why_it_matters: LD provisions (up to 5× annual royalty) may not be enforceable in CA

overrides.override_summary: Termination upon bankruptcy may not be enforceable under federal law
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects franchisee from automatic termination in bankruptcy

overrides.override_summary: Illinois law governs the franchise agreements
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides NC choice of law

overrides.override_summary: Venue designation outside Illinois is void (but arbitration outside IL permitted)
overrides.why_it_matters: IL franchisees cannot be forced to litigate in NC

overrides.override_summary: Anti-waiver: no statement signed can waive claims under IL franchise law or disclaim reliance on franchisor statements
overrides.why_it_matters: Fraud in the inducement claims preserved

overrides.override_summary: Release does not apply to claims under Indiana Deceptive Franchise Practices Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisee retains right to bring claims for deceptive practices

overrides.override_summary: NC venue may not be enforceable under Indiana law
overrides.why_it_matters: IN franchisee may be able to sue in Indiana

overrides.override_summary: Indiana franchise laws govern all agreements
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides NC choice of law

overrides.override_summary: Release does not apply to claims under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchise law claims preserved in renewal/transfer releases

overrides.override_summary: May bring suit in Maryland for MD franchise law claims
overrides.why_it_matters: Avoids NC venue for MD-specific claims

overrides.override_summary: Claims under MD franchise law must be brought within 3 years after grant of franchise
overrides.why_it_matters: Sets clear limitations period

overrides.override_summary: 90 days notice of termination required (60 days to cure); 180 days non-renewal notice. Good cause required for termination.
overrides.why_it_matters: Significantly extends cure periods and requires good cause — protects against rapid termination

overrides.override_summary: Release does not apply to claims under Minnesota Franchise Act
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchise act claims preserved

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require litigation outside Minnesota or waiver of jury trial
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects right to litigate in MN with jury

overrides.override_summary: Cannot consent to liquidated damages, termination penalties, or judgment notes
overrides.why_it_matters: LD provisions may be unenforceable in MN

overrides.override_summary: Franchise Act protections apply to non-competition provisions
overrides.why_it_matters: N/A for this FDD — no noncompete exists

overrides.override_summary: Release subject to GBL Article 33 protections — non-waiver of rights thereunder
overrides.why_it_matters: NY franchise law rights cannot be waived via general release

overrides.override_summary: Choice of law cannot waive rights conferred by GBL Article 33
overrides.why_it_matters: NY law applies alongside NC law for NY franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Release does not apply to extent prohibited by ND Franchise Investment Law
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchise law claims preserved

overrides.override_summary: ND Securities Commissioner has held liquidated damages and termination penalties unfair, unjust, and inequitable
overrides.why_it_matters: LD provisions likely unenforceable in ND

overrides.override_summary: May bring action in North Dakota; mediation at mutually agreed site
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees can litigate locally

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require waiver of jury trial or waiver of exemplary/punitive damages
overrides.why_it_matters: Preserves jury trial and punitive damages rights

overrides.override_summary: Venue outside RI and application of non-RI law void for RI Franchise Investment Act claims
overrides.why_it_matters: RI franchisees protected from out-of-state proceedings

overrides.override_summary: Franchisor cannot cancel franchise without reasonable cause per VA Retail Franchising Act Section 13.1-564
overrides.why_it_matters: Additional termination protection

overrides.override_summary: Washington Franchise Investment Protection Act prevails in conflicts
overrides.why_it_matters: WA law overrides FA provisions

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration/mediation must be in Washington or mutually agreed location; litigation for WA franchise act claims in WA
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchisees can resolve disputes locally

overrides.override_summary: Release cannot include rights under WA Franchise Investment Protection Act except in negotiated post-agreement settlement with independent counsel
overrides.why_it_matters: Strong protection of WA franchise act rights

overrides.override_summary: Noncompete void unless employee earns >$100K/year or contractor >$250K/year (adjusted for inflation). No-poach provisions void.
overrides.why_it_matters: N/A for this FDD (no noncompete) but no-poach prohibition is notable

overrides.override_summary: Transfer fees collectible only to extent they reflect reasonable estimated or actual costs
overrides.why_it_matters: May limit the then-current IFF transfer fee if it exceeds actual costs
